location.office_time_required: 3 days per week (Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays); hybrid roles have flexibility to work from anywhere for up to 4 weeks per year

level_signals: 5+ years of program management or operational leadership required
0-to-1 build-out ownership and strategic structuring responsibilities
Base comp range $108,240 - $135,300 consistent with senior individual contributor

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of program management or operational leadership within the Autonomous Vehicle (AV), Electric Vehicle (EV), or Advanced Mobility sectors.
Proven experience launching new service centers from scratch. Must possess deep expertise in managing complex launch timelines, facility layouts, permitting, and equipment procurement.
Solid understanding of AV sensor systems (lidar, radar, cameras), EV high-voltage systems, and automotive service operations to effectively build technical frameworks.
Proficiency managing high-velocity operations using data, Fleet Management Systems (FMS), ticketing systems, and data visualization (Tableau/Excel).
Ability to travel up to 50% of the time, particularly during peak depot launch phases.

Establish rigorous project management organization, operating rhythms, and cross-functional stakeholder reporting. Own master launch timelines, critical path dependencies, and risk mitigation strategies for all new depot build-outs.

Develop, document, and continuously optimize Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs). Translate AV/EV and OEM technical requirements into streamlined, highly efficient repair workflows for the shop floor.

Collaborate tightly with Biz Ops and Finance to develop strategic financial models for facility stand-ups. Forecast CapEx/OpEx, model ROI for tooling investments, and build foundational reporting dashboards to track operational efficiency (FTFR, Cycle Time) during the launch phase.
Partner with Regulatory and EHS teams to ensure 100% compliance with AV technical standards, overseeing High Voltage (HV) safety protocols (NFPA 70E, LOTO) and hazardous waste management.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Certifications: PMP, Agile, Six Sigma/Lean, or I-Car Certification.

scope.surface: AV service depot build-out, technical operations infrastructure, technician workforce establishment for autonomous vehicle fleet
scope.business_model: B2B fleet management / AV fleet operations (subsidiary of Lyft)
primary_metrics_inferred: Operational readiness / go-live criteria for depot launches
core_responsibilities: Establish program governance, operating rhythms, and cross-functional stakeholder reporting
Own master launch timelines, critical path dependencies, and risk mitigation for depot build-outs
Define operational readiness criteria and lead handoff to Fleet Repair Ops team
Partner with Recruiting to define AV technician hiring profile and competency benchmarks
Design, build, and implement technical training and certification program for technicians
Develop and optimize SOPs and repair workflows for AV/EV systems
Collaborate with Real Estate and Procurement on maintenance bay design, tooling sourcing, and SLA negotiation
Architect parts management software and inventory control system integrated with Fleet Management Systems
Develop strategic financial models, forecast CapEx/OpEx, model ROI, and build reporting dashboards
Develop scalable launch playbook for future market expansion
Ensure compliance with AV technical standards, HV safety protocols, and hazardous waste management

compensation.equity_form: potential equity offering mentioned; details not specified

green_flags: Clear 0-to-1 ownership scope with defined handoff criteria — not a vague role
Flexdrive is a Lyft subsidiary giving stability of a larger parent company
Hybrid schedule clearly defined (3 days/week, specific days named)
Up to 4 weeks/year flexibility to work from anywhere
Cross-functional scope is broad and well-articulated — high visibility role
red_flags: Up to 50% travel required, particularly during peak depot launch phases — significant lifestyle constraint
Office not yet established in Nashville — hybrid schedule contingent on 'establishment of a Lyft office in Nashville', timing unclear
Visa sponsorship not mentioned — unclear for international candidates
Role is a 0-to-1 builder position with eventual handoff to another team, which may limit long-term ownership satisfaction
